scripts/timing-bench.sh implements full protocol. TIMING_BENCH_MODELS default verbatim match. Native tool_calls check (line 114-115) excludes text-formatted. Locked 2026-04-11 measurements drive model-tier defaults.
Companion annotations file per ANNOTATION-SYSTEMS.org §3 schema covering all docs not carrying inline drawers (release notes, ADRs, RFCs, reports, operational + contract docs, top-level repo docs).
Local mirror of wal.sh/_queue/_drafts convention. 10 queue items (Q01-Q10) tracking deep-audit/extension/spec-decision/tooling work that exceeded this pass's budget.

§3 macOS footnote confirmed empirically: (allow network-outbound (remote tcp localhost:*)) LEAKED to LAN (direct $LITELLM_HOST -> HTTP 200). FS confinement via (deny file-read* HOME) overlay is solid. Full (deny network*) reliable. Localhost narrowing unreliable -> recommend UDS bridge.
_queue location decision: hybrid (docs/_queue/ for PR-review; XDG for ephemeral agent scratch); maps to wal.sh _queue vs _drafts split.

Live broker->mint->handoff->sandbox-use->revoke->verify-401 cycle ran end-to-end with real ephemeral key (gemini-2.5-flash, $0.01 budget, 120s TTL) on LITELLM_HOST=$LITELLM_HOST. HC-01/04/07, CC-01/02/03, N4 demonstrated.

§9 LiteLLM hardening: silent clamp upperbound, async revoke vs auth cache, fail-open guardrails on provider errors, multi-pod spend drift, 2026 CVEs (-42208 SQLi exploited in wild fixed 1.83.7, -49468 host-header auth bypass fixed 1.84.0), PyPI 1.82.7/1.82.8 credential-harvesting malware (GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76). Min bar: litellm >= 1.84.0 pinned by hash.

Added §3 matrix row (f) Docker Sandboxes (sbx CLI): hypervisor microVM, sbx policy allow network deny-default, sbx secret set + host-side proxy credential injection (sandbox never sees raw token, SPIFFE-shaped). Strictly stronger than Docker Desktop row (d).

§10 cross-agent applicability: same SessionSpec+CredentialBundle contract, different attach() per agent. Tabulated guile-sage / Claude Code CLI / OpenAI Codex / sbx run. sbx + sbx secret is best-HC-04 (sandbox never reads raw token).
Renamed 'coordinator' -> 'Issuer' (OIDC-style) across RFC-003 (7 occurrences) and SANDBOX-HARNESS-CONTRACT.org (11). CC-01..04 codes retained as stable identifiers.

Provisioning sub-agent created 3 real keys: dsp-dr-personal (30d/$5/all models), guile-builder-agent-key (7d/$0.50/4 models, team=guile-builder), ephemeral-session (15m/$0.02). All /v1/models + /key/info HTTP 200. Cleanup script + READMEs in tmp/litellm-provision/ (gitignored).

Field report drafted to wal.sh repo _drafts/ confirming the four-axis sandbox decomposition empirically; three findings: (1) secret-custody contract works via tmpfs handoff, (2) SBPL localhost-narrowing leaks LAN, (3) LiteLLM broker stability/CVE landscape. Proposes Issuer nomenclature. Left in _drafts/ for jwalsh to commit.

Deno permission runtime (v2.8.3) ran the same handoff probe: HTTP 200 on /v1/models + chat under --allow-net=$LITELLM_HOST --allow-read=/tmp/sbx-exp/.key-handoff. Negative probe (fetch example.com + read $HOME/.zsh_history) both NotCapable. Revoke -> 401. Deno's --allow-net narrows egress reliably where SBPL §8.4 variant 4 leaks LAN.

Added §3 matrix row (g) Deno permission runtime: declarative permissions at exec time, host:port-exact --allow-net, cross-platform. Sandbox scope is JS/TS only — spawning native subprocesses needs --allow-run and child runs outside the cage.
App Sandbox dismissed: requires .app bundle + Info.plist + entitlements + code signing — wrong shape for a CLI agent. Practical macOS native paths are sandbox-exec (row c) and Deno (row g).
